'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|ja|千葉氏}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} (present-day {{ill|Omi Village, Nagano|ja|麻績村}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} (present-day {{ill|Omi Village, Nagano|ja|麻績村}}, Nagano Prefecture). The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}

revision.text:
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|ja|千葉氏}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} (present-day {{ill|Omi Village, Nagano|ja|麻績村}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|ja|千葉氏}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} (present-day {{ill|Omi Village, Nagano|ja|麻績村}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}

The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}

{{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}} '''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|ja|千葉氏}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国}} (present-day {{ill|Omi Village, Nagano|ja|麻績村}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

revision.text:
'''小見'''、'''麻績'''（おみ）氏は、日本の氏族の一つ。
== 平姓小見氏 == [[下総国]][[香取郡]][[小見郷]]（現・[[千葉県]][[香取市]]小見）発祥の小見氏（'''小海氏'''）は[[房総平氏]]の氏族で、以下の流れがある。 * [[匝瑳氏|匝瑳党]]小見氏と呼ばれる[[上総氏]]の流れ * [[千葉常胤]]の弟[[小見胤隆|胤隆]]の流れ * [[椎名胤光]]の子[[小見胤澄|胤澄]]の流れ * [[東胤頼]]の子[[木内胤朝]]の孫[[小見胤直|胤直]]の流れ [[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[千葉氏]]のもとで歴史に名を残した。
== 源姓麻績氏 == '''麻績氏'''（小見氏）は、[[信濃国]][[筑摩郡]][[麻績郷]]（現・[[長野県]][[東筑摩郡]][[麻績村]]）の豪族。その始まりは[[鎌倉時代]]に遡り、[[小笠原長親 (四郎)|小笠原長親]]が麻績の[[地頭]]となり麻績氏を称した。即ち[[小笠原氏]]の庶流であり、[[阿波小笠原氏]]や[[石見小笠原氏]]と同祖である。
このことは「[[中興武家系図]]」に、 :麻績、[[清和源氏|清和]]、本國[[信濃国|信濃]]、[[小笠原長房 (阿波小笠原氏)|小笠原阿波守長房]]の男、四郎長親これを称す。 とあることからも窺える。
一族の詳しい動向や系図は不明だが、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の[[側室]]で[[武田勝頼]]の[[おばあさん|祖母]]にあたる女性は小笠原の家臣 小見（麻績）氏の娘とされている。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]は、麻績氏は[[武田信玄]]の信濃侵攻によって没落し、麻績氏の一族と伝わる[[青柳氏]]の[[青柳清長]]が信玄より麻績氏の名跡を与えられて[[麻績城]]に入り、[[青柳清長|麻績清長]]と名乗った（[[麻績青柳氏]]）。[[武田氏]]配下時代は10騎の軍役で活動したとされている。
[[生島足島神社]]に残された武田信玄配下多数の[[起請文]]の中に麻績清永の物は２通あって異彩を放っている。前日付の決まりきった内容と異なる翌日の再提出文は国侍同士で仲良くしないこと。特に互いに元[[村上氏]]の配下であって領地を接している[[屋代氏]]や[[室賀氏]]、[[大日方氏]]とは殊更仲良くしないことを誓わされている。
こうして麻績氏は戦国時代を通じて存続し、天正十年（[[1582年]]）の[[甲州征伐|武田氏滅亡]]の後も続き、当主[[青柳清長|頼長]]（清長の息子）は[[上杉景勝]]、後に[[小笠原貞慶]]に仕えたが天正十五年（[[1587年]]）[[9月28日]]、[[松本城|松本城内]]にて子息の[[麻績長迪|長迪]]とともに誅殺され、麻績氏は滅亡した。
== 藤原姓小見氏 == '''小見（麻績）氏'''は、[[藤原秀郷]]を祖とする[[足利氏 (藤原氏)|足利氏]]の庶流[[佐野氏]]の流れをくむ氏族である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の末に、[[佐野秀綱]]の弟是綱が[[下野国]][[安蘇郡]]麻績郷（現・[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]小見）を領し'''[[小見是綱]]'''と名乗ったことが始まり。
また、[[武蔵国]][[埼玉郡]][[小見邑]]（現・[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]小見）発祥の小見氏とは同族である。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[小見]]（[[麻績]]）
{{DEFAULTSORT:おみし}} [[Category:小見氏|*]] [[Category:麻績氏|*]] [[Category:房総平氏|*]] [[Category:小笠原氏|*]] [[Category:佐野氏|*]] [[Category:下総国の氏族]] [[Category:信濃国の氏族]] [[Category:下野国の氏族]] [[Category:日本の氏族]] [[Category:香取郡|氏おみ]]

revision.text:
'''小見'''、'''麻績'''（おみ）氏は、日本の氏族の一つ。
== 平姓小見氏 == [[下総国]][[香取郡]][[小見郷]]（現・[[千葉県]][[香取市]]小見）発祥の小見氏（'''小海氏'''）は[[房総平氏]]の氏族で、以下の流れがある。 * [[匝瑳氏|匝瑳党]]小見氏と呼ばれる[[上総氏]]の流れ * [[千葉常胤]]の弟[[小見胤隆|胤隆]]の流れ * [[椎名胤光]]の子[[小見胤澄|胤澄]]の流れ * [[東胤頼]]の子[[木内胤朝]]の孫[[小見胤直|胤直]]の流れ [[鎌倉時代]]以降、[[千葉氏]]のもとで歴史に名を残した。
== 源姓麻績氏 == '''麻績氏'''（小見氏）は、[[信濃国]][[筑摩郡]][[麻績郷]]（現・[[長野県]][[東筑摩郡]][[麻績村]]）の豪族。その始まりは[[鎌倉時代]]に遡り、[[小笠原長親 (四郎)|小笠原長親]]が麻績の[[地頭]]となり麻績氏を称した。即ち[[小笠原氏]]の庶流であり、[[阿波小笠原氏]]や[[石見小笠原氏]]と同祖である。
このことは「[[中興武家系図]]」に、 :麻績、[[清和源氏|清和]]、本國[[信濃国|信濃]]、[[小笠原長房 (阿波小笠原氏)|小笠原阿波守長房]]の男、四郎長親これを称す。 とあることからも窺える。
一族の詳しい動向や系図は不明だが、[[諏訪頼重 (戦国時代)|諏訪頼重]]の[[側室]]で[[武田勝頼]]の[[おばあさん|祖母]]にあたる女性は小笠原の家臣 小見（麻績）氏の娘とされている。
[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]は、麻績氏は[[武田信玄]]の信濃侵攻によって没落し、麻績氏の一族と伝わる[[青柳氏]]の[[青柳清長]]が信玄より麻績氏の名跡を与えられて[[麻績城]]に入り、[[青柳清長|麻績清長]]と名乗った（[[麻績青柳氏]]）。[[武田氏]]配下時代は10騎の軍役で活動したとされている。
[[生島足島神社]]に残された武田信玄配下多数の[[起請文]]の中に麻績清永の物は２通あって異彩を放っている。前日付の決まりきった内容と異なる翌日の再提出文は国侍同士で仲良くしないこと。特に互いに元[[村上氏]]の配下であって領地を接している[[屋代氏]]や[[室賀氏]]、[[大日方氏]]とは殊更仲良くしないことを誓わされている。
こうして麻績氏は戦国時代を通じて存続し、天正十年（[[1582年]]）の[[甲州征伐|武田氏滅亡]]の後も続き、当主[[青柳清長|頼長]]（清長の息子）は[[上杉景勝]]、後に[[小笠原貞慶]]に仕えたが天正十五年（[[1587年]]）[[9月28日]]、[[松本城|松本城内]]にて子息の[[麻績長迪|長迪]]とともに誅殺され、麻績氏は滅亡した。
== 藤原姓小見氏 == '''小見（麻績）氏'''は、[[藤原秀郷]]を祖とする[[足利氏 (藤原氏)|足利氏]]の庶流[[佐野氏]]の流れをくむ氏族である。[[戦国時代 (日本)|戦国時代]]の末に、[[佐野秀綱]]の弟是綱が[[下野国]][[安蘇郡]]麻績郷（現・[[栃木県]][[佐野市]]小見）を領し'''[[小見是綱]]'''と名乗ったことが始まり。
また、[[武蔵国]][[埼玉郡]][[小見邑]]（現・[[埼玉県]][[行田市]]小見）発祥の小見氏とは同族である。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[小見]]（[[麻績]]）

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|qq=draft}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|qq=draft}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|ja|千葉氏|qq=draft}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Omi Village, Nagano|ja|麻績村|qq=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏|qq=draft}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|qq=draft}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼|qq=draft}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社|qq=draft}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝|qq=draft}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市|qq=draft}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市|qq=draft}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Omi (Momi)]]

{{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|qq=}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Province|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori, Chiba|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|qq=}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|qq=}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|qq=}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|qq=}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|qq=}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|ja|千葉氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba clan|qq=}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Omi Village, Nagano|ja|麻績村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Village, Nagano|qq=}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|qq=}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara clan|qq=}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|qq=}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorishige|qq=}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Katsuyori|qq=}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|qq=}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|qq=}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|qq=}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|qq=}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|qq=}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Kagekatsu|qq=}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōda, Saitama|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|qq=}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Kagekatsu|qq=}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōda, Saitama|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|qq=}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|ja|下総国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimōsa Province|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|ja|香取市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katori, Chiba|qq=}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|qq=}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|qq=}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|qq=}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|qq=}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|qq=}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|ja|千葉氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba clan|qq=}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|qq=}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|ja|信濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Province|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Omi Village, Nagano|ja|麻績村|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Village, Nagano|qq=}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|ja|鎌倉時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamakura period|qq=}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|qq=}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|ja|小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara clan|qq=}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|qq=}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|ja|諏訪頼重|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Yorishige|qq=}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|ja|武田勝頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Katsuyori|qq=}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|qq=}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|qq=}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|qq=}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|qq=}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|ja|生島足島神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|qq=}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|ja|上杉景勝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uesugi Kagekatsu|qq=}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|ja|藤原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara clan|qq=}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代 (日本)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|ja|佐野市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano, Tochigi|qq=}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda, Saitama|ja|行田市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōda, Saitama|qq=}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|qq=}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|qq=}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|qq=}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|qq=}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}.
After the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the Omi clan remained historically significant under the [[:en:Chiba clan|Chiba clan]].
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (present-day [[:en:Omi, Nagano|Omi Village, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|qq=}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|qq=}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]] and the grandmother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|qq=}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|qq=}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|qq=}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|qq=}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|qq=}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|qq=}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|qq=}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|qq=}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|qq=}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}.
After the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the Omi clan remained historically significant under the [[:en:Chiba clan|Chiba clan]].
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (present-day [[:en:Omi, Nagano|Omi Village, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|qq=}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|qq=}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]] and the grandmother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|qq=}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|qq=}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|qq=}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|qq=}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Omi (Momi)]]

{{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|qq=}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|qq=}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|qq=}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|qq=}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|qq=}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}.
After the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the Omi clan remained historically significant under the [[:en:Chiba clan|Chiba clan]].
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (present-day [[:en:Omi, Nagano|Omi Village, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|qq=}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|qq=}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]] and the grandmother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|qq=}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|qq=}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|qq=}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|qq=}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Omi (Momi)]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|qq=}} in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|qq=}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|qq=}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|qq=}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|qq=}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|qq=}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=}}.
After the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the Omi clan remained historically significant under the [[:en:Chiba clan|Chiba clan]].
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|qq=}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (present-day [[:en:Omi, Nagano|Omi Village, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|qq=}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|qq=}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|qq=}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]] and the grandmother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|qq=}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|qq=}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|qq=}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|qq=}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Omi (Momi)]]

}}

[[sn:Omi clan (麻績氏)]] [[ja:小見氏]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Bōsō Taira clan
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|qq=}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|qq=}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|qq=}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|qq=}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|qq=}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the Omi clan remained historically significant under the [[:en:Chiba clan|Chiba clan]].
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (present-day [[:en:Omi, Nagano|Omi Village, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|qq=}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]] and the grandmother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|qq=}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Omi (Momi)]]

== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|qq=}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|qq=}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|qq=}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|qq=}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|qq=}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|qq=}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the Omi clan remained historically significant under the [[:en:Chiba clan|Chiba clan]].
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (present-day [[:en:Omi, Nagano|Omi Village, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|qq=}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]] and the grandmother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to {{ill|Takeda Shingen|ja|Takeda Shingen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|qq=}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|qq=}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|qq=}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|qq=}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|qq=}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|qq=}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|qq=}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|qq=}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
