'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|1=Bōsō Taira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496703}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|1=Sōsa Party Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864171}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406057}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|1=Shiina Tanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864441}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|1=Taneyori Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528198}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the Omi clan remained historically significant under the [[:en:Chiba clan|Chiba clan]].
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (present-day [[:en:Omi, Nagano|Omi Village, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|1=Ogasawara Nagachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30935702}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975340}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]] and the grandmother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|1=Momi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677623}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Yashiro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|1=Momi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677623}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Yashiro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11463661]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (present-day [[:en:Katori, Chiba|Katori, Chiba]], Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|1=Bōsō Taira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496703}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|1=Sōsa Party Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864171}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406057}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|1=Shiina Tanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864441}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|1=Taneyori Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528198}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], the Omi clan remained historically significant under the [[:en:Chiba clan|Chiba clan]].
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (present-day [[:en:Omi, Nagano|Omi Village, Nagano]], Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|1=Ogasawara Nagachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30935702}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the [[:en:Ogasawara clan|Ogasawara clan]], sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975340}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of [[:en:Suwa Yorishige|Suwa Yorishige]] and the grandmother of [[:en:Takeda Katsuyori|Takeda Katsuyori]].
During the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]], the Momi clan declined due to [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|1=Momi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677623}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The [[:en:Ikushimatarushima Shrine|Ikushima Tarushima Shrine]] archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Yashiro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served [[:en:Uesugi Kagekatsu|Uesugi Kagekatsu]] and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the [[:en:Fujiwara clan|Fujiwara clan]] originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Sano, Tochigi|Sano, Tochigi]], Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day [[:en:Gyōda|Gyōda, Saitama]], Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11463661]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:46, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=unclear how to do the title[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 23:43, 31 March 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|1=Bōsō Taira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496703}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|1=Sōsa Party Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864171}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406057}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|1=Shiina Tanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864441}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|1=Taneyori Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528198}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|en|Chiba clan|WD=Q2975237|lt=Chiba clan}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} (present-day {{ill|Omi, Nagano|en|Omi, Nagano|WD=Q1349060|lt=Omi Village, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|1=Ogasawara Nagachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30935702}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975340}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the Momi clan declined due to [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|1=Momi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677623}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Yashiro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|en|Uesugi Kagekatsu|WD=Q1376605|lt=Uesugi Kagekatsu}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|en|Sano, Tochigi|WD=Q646912|lt=Sano, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]]
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|1=Bōsō Taira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496703}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|1=Sōsa Party Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864171}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406057}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|1=Shiina Tanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864441}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|1=Taneyori Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528198}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|en|Chiba clan|WD=Q2975237|lt=Chiba clan}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} (present-day {{ill|Omi, Nagano|en|Omi, Nagano|WD=Q1349060|lt=Omi Village, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|1=Ogasawara Nagachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30935702}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975340}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the Momi clan declined due to [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|1=Momi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677623}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Yashiro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|en|Uesugi Kagekatsu|WD=Q1376605|lt=Uesugi Kagekatsu}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|en|Sano, Tochigi|WD=Q646912|lt=Sano, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Omi (Momi)]]

== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|1=Bōsō Taira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496703}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|1=Sōsa Party Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864171}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406057}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|1=Shiina Tanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864441}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|1=Taneyori Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528198}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|en|Chiba clan|WD=Q2975237|lt=Chiba clan}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} (present-day {{ill|Omi, Nagano|en|Omi, Nagano|WD=Q1349060|lt=Omi Village, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|1=Ogasawara Nagachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30935702}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975340}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori}}.

During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Yashiro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|en|Uesugi Kagekatsu|WD=Q1376605|lt=Uesugi Kagekatsu}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|en|Sano, Tochigi|WD=Q646912|lt=Sano, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975340}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}}, the Momi clan declined due to [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|1=Momi Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677623}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Yashiro clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|en|Uesugi Kagekatsu|WD=Q1376605|lt=Uesugi Kagekatsu}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|en|Sano, Tochigi|WD=Q646912|lt=Sano, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]]
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|1=Q11496703|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496703}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|1=Q24864171|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864171}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406057}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|1=Shiina Tanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864441}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|1=Taneyori Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528198}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|en|Chiba clan|WD=Q2975237|lt=Chiba clan|1=Chiba clan}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} (present-day {{ill|Omi, Nagano|en|Omi, Nagano|WD=Q1349060|lt=Omi Village, Nagano|1=Omi, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|1=Ogasawara Nagachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30935702}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan|1=Ogasawara clan}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975340}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|1=Takeda Katsuyori}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the Momi clan declined due to [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|1=Q11677623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677623}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Q11465659|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|en|Uesugi Kagekatsu|WD=Q1376605|lt=Uesugi Kagekatsu|1=Uesugi Kagekatsu}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|en|Sano, Tochigi|WD=Q646912|lt=Sano, Tochigi|1=Sano, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama|1=Gyōda}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

The {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Q11465659|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|en|Uesugi Kagekatsu|WD=Q1376605|lt=Uesugi Kagekatsu|1=Uesugi Kagekatsu}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|en|Sano, Tochigi|WD=Q646912|lt=Sano, Tochigi|1=Sano, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama|1=Gyōda}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:小見氏]]
'''Omi (Omi/Momi) clan''' (小見, 麻績) is one of the clans of Japan.
== Hei Clan Omi == The Omi clan ('''Komi clan''') originating from {{ill|Omi-go, Katori District|ja|小見郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-go, Katori District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}} (present-day {{ill|Katori, Chiba|en|Katori, Chiba|WD=Q858357|lt=Katori, Chiba|1=Katori, Chiba}}, Chiba Prefecture) belonged to the {{ill|Bōsō Taira|ja|房総平氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bōsō Taira|1=Q11496703|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11496703}} lineage. Several branches emerged: * The {{ill|Sōsa Party Omi|ja|匝瑳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōsa Party Omi|1=Q24864171|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864171}} clan, stemming from the {{ill|Kazusa clan|ja|上総氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kazusa clan|1=Kazusa clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11360044}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Chiba Tsunetane|ja|千葉常胤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Tsunetane|1=Chiba Tsunetane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11406057}}’s younger brother {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤隆|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Shiina Tademitsu|ja|椎名胤光|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiina Tademitsu|1=Shiina Tanemitsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24864441}}’s son {{ill|Omi Tanesumi|ja|小見胤澄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanesumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * The lineage of {{ill|Higashi Tadakazu|ja|東胤頼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashi Tadakazu|1=Taneyori Tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11528198}}’s grandson {{ill|Omi Tanechika|ja|小見胤直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Tanechika|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the Omi clan remained historically significant under the {{ill|Chiba clan|en|Chiba clan|WD=Q2975237|lt=Chiba clan|1=Chiba clan}}.
== Minamoto Clan Momi == The '''Momi clan''' (Omi clan) were local lords of {{ill|Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi-gō, Chikuma District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province|1=Shinano Province}} (present-day {{ill|Omi, Nagano|en|Omi, Nagano|WD=Q1349060|lt=Omi Village, Nagano|1=Omi, Nagano}}, Nagano Prefecture). Their origins date back to the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, when {{ill|Ogasawara Nagachika|ja|小笠原長親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Nagachika|1=Ogasawara Nagachika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30935702}} became the estate steward of Momi and adopted the Momi name. The clan was a branch of the {{ill|Ogasawara clan|en|Ogasawara clan|WD=Q2975340|lt=Ogasawara clan|1=Ogasawara clan}}, sharing ancestry with the {{ill|Awa Ogasawara clan|ja|阿波小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小笠原氏|1=Ogasawara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975340}} and the {{ill|Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja|石見小笠原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwami Ogasawara clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
This connection is noted in the "Chūkō Buke Keizu": :"Momi, Minamoto clan, based in Shinano. Descended from Ogasawara Awa-no-Kami Nagafusa's son, Shirō Nagachika."
One member of the Momi clan became a concubine of {{ill|Suwa Yorishige|en|Suwa Yorishige|WD=Q1213900|lt=Suwa Yorishige|1=Suwa Yorishige}} and the grandmother of {{ill|Takeda Katsuyori|en|Takeda Katsuyori|WD=Q662482|lt=Takeda Katsuyori|1=Takeda Katsuyori}}.
During the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, the Momi clan declined due to [[Takeda Shingen]]{{efn|Japanese feudal lord (1521–1573)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeda Shingen|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}’s invasion of Shinano. {{ill|Aoyagi clan|ja|青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} leader {{ill|Aoyagi Kiyonaga|ja|青柳清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Kiyonaga|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}} was granted the Momi name by Shingen and became known as {{ill|Momi Kiyonaga|ja|麻績清長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Kiyonaga|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 青柳清長|1=Aoyagi Kiyonaga|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11661951}}, taking residence in {{ill|Momi Castle|ja|麻績城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Castle|1=Q11677623|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677623}} ({{ill|Momi Aoyagi clan|ja|麻績青柳氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Aoyagi clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}). During this period, the Momi clan served the Takeda with military contributions of 10 horsemen.
The {{ill|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|en|Ikushimatarushima Shrine|WD=Q11574513|lt=Ikushima Tarushima Shrine|1=Ikushimatarushima Shrine}} archives contain oaths signed by Takeda vassals, including two from Momi Kiyoshige, showing a focus on maintaining enmity towards neighboring former {{ill|Murakami clan|ja|村上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Murakami clan|1=Murakami clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522833}} vassals such as the {{ill|Yashiro clan|ja|屋代氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yashiro clan|1=Q11465659|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11465659}} and {{ill|Muroga clan|ja|室賀氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muroga clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
After the fall of the Takeda in 1582 during the {{ill|Campaign against the Takeda|ja|甲州征伐|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Campaign against the Takeda|1=Kōshū Conquest|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55713231}}, the clan persisted. The head of the Momi clan, {{ill|Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja|頼長|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aoyagi Yoshinaga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, served {{ill|Uesugi Kagekatsu|en|Uesugi Kagekatsu|WD=Q1376605|lt=Uesugi Kagekatsu|1=Uesugi Kagekatsu}} and later {{ill|Ogasawara Sadayoshi|ja|小笠原貞慶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ogasawara Sadayoshi|1=Ogasawara Sadayoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10856562}}. However, in 1587, Yoshinaga and his son {{ill|Momi Nagamichi|ja|麻績長迪|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momi Nagamichi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} were executed, leading to the end of the clan.
== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|en|Sano, Tochigi|WD=Q646912|lt=Sano, Tochigi|1=Sano, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama|1=Gyōda}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.

== Fujiwara Clan Omi == Another Omi clan claimed descent from the {{ill|Sano clan (Fujiwara)|ja|佐野氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano clan (Fujiwara)|1=Sano clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385058}}, a branch of the {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|WD=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara clan|1=Fujiwara clan}} originating from {{ill|Fujiwara Hidesato|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1136623}}. This branch arose during the late {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} when {{ill|Sano Koretsuna|ja|佐野是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a brother of {{ill|Sano Shigetaka|ja|佐野秀綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sano Shigetaka|1=Sano Hidetsuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11385090}}, settled in {{ill|Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja|麻績郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi-gō, Ashigaru District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Sano, Tochigi|en|Sano, Tochigi|WD=Q646912|lt=Sano, Tochigi|1=Sano, Tochigi}}, Tochigi Prefecture) and adopted the name {{ill|Omi Koretsuna|ja|小見是綱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi Koretsuna|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, an Omi clan from {{ill|Omi village, Musashi Province|ja|小見邑|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Omi village, Musashi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (present-day {{ill|Gyōda|en|Gyōda|WD=Q860659|lt=Gyōda, Saitama|1=Gyōda}}, Saitama Prefecture) shares lineage with this family.
